title: rhus radicans subsp. negundo
intro: Rhus radicans subsp. negundo, also known as the Velvet Stiltgrass or Manchineel Vine, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds like tannins and flavonoids. The plant has been reported to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating skin conditions, and exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. In terms of herbal preparations, Rhus radicans subsp. negundo is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which are applied topically to affected areas or consumed orally to leverage its medicinal properties.
